title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
summary: This tutorial presents a system design interview methodology that reverses conventional approaches by prioritizing problem requirements over technology selection. The framework consists of seven sequential steps: understanding the problem, identifying requirements, predicting traffic patterns, designing high-level components, defining APIs, selecting a tech stack, and considering implementation constraints.
writeup: This tutorial lays out a system design interview methodology that inverts the common approach by putting problem requirements ahead of technology selection. The framework runs through seven sequential steps: understanding the problem, identifying requirements, predicting traffic patterns, designing high-level components, defining APIs, selecting a tech stack, and considering implementation constraints. Ordering technology choice near the end matters because it forces decisions to follow from defined requirements and estimated traffic rather than the reverse. The coverage also touches on failure analysis and traffic estimation as part of the process.
